text: We present a semantic parser that can handle the scale of Freebase. Instead of relying on expensive annotations of logical forms, we train the parser from question-answer pairs. The main challenge is to identify a reasonable set of logical predicates for each question that the parser can choose from. We address this challenge with two techniques. First, we learn a coarse mapping between phrases and predicates using Freebase and a large text corpus. Second, we train a special bridging operation to generate additional predicates that connect neighboring predicates. Without access to the annotated logical forms (which is a costly resource), our parser still achieves a better performance on the Cai and Yates dataset than the state-of-the-art parser. We also build a new dataset of question-answer pairs that are more human-behavior-driven and more challenging to tackle, and we show that our parser also has a better performance than a simple natural baseline.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
